scope_boundary: Typed job durable lifecycle probe only. The probe validates two deterministic synthetic durable lifecycle traces and rejects nine expected-invalid controls for retry without idempotency, authority widening on retry, permission overreach after resume, expired-lease dispatch, missing completion receipt, missing replay ref, missing residual owner, missing non-claim boundary, and support-state promotion from fixture shape. It does not execute a deployed scheduler, durable workflow engine, permission enforcement service, approval service, runtime adapter, replay engine, lifecycle checker, completion-receipt service, benchmark, model-quality evaluation, safety evaluation, or working Labor OS runtime.
transition_reason: The typed job durable lifecycle probe is useful because it makes retry/resume, idempotency, authority preservation, permission basis, lease blocking, completion receipts, replay refs, residual ownership, non-claim boundaries, and support-state non-promotion executable as finite Labor OS records. It remains a deterministic synthetic record gate, not deployed Labor OS evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for deployed scheduler behavior, durable workflow recovery, permission enforcement, approval-service behavior, adapter execution, replay correctness, completion-receipt service behavior, live workflow traces, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, ASI, or the Labor OS chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable typed-job lifecycle checker trace
replay engine trace with replay-correctness checks
deployed scheduler or externally replayed workflow trace
runtime evidence across real or externally reviewable typed jobs
independent review of Labor OS execution boundaries
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: authority widening on retry is rejected
permission overreach after resume is rejected
evidence-ready durable job without completion receipt is rejected
evidence-ready durable job without replay ref is rejected
blocked durable trace without residual owner is rejected
support-state promotion from fixture shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: Typed job durable lifecycle probe validator failure

retry without idempotency, authority widening, permission overreach, expired-lease dispatch, missing completion receipt, missing replay ref, missing residual owner, missing non-claim boundary, or support-promotion control accepted as valid

probe described as deployed scheduler, durable workflow recovery, permission-enforcement, approval-service, adapter-runner, completion-receipt service, replay-correctness, benchmark, model-quality, safety, ASI, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed Labor OS durable lifecycle claim after deployed or externally reviewable typed-job lifecycle checking, tool-permission enforcement, approval-service behavior, adapter-runner execution, completion-receipt service behavior, replay-engine correctness, durable workflow recovery, scheduler or externally replayed workflow traces, runtime evidence over real or externally reviewable jobs, residual accounting, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: deterministic synthetic durable lifecycle fixture only
no replay engine or replay-correctness result
no live or externally replayed workflow trace
does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent Labor OS, workflow-engine, permission-service, approval-service, adapter-runner, replay-engine, scheduler, benchmark, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable typed-job lifecycle checker trace
no live or externally replayed workflow traces
no independent Labor OS execution review
non_claims: does not promote typed job durable lifecycle evidence above argument

does not prove deployed scheduler behavior, durable workflow recovery, permission enforcement, approval-service behavior, adapter execution, completion-receipt service behavior, replay correctness, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Labor OS and Typed Jobs chapter core claim
